tests/features/git-history.test.ts.note: This file crashes Bun 1.3.14 on this machine with "panic(main thread): A C++ exception occurred" during embedding (onnx native), reproduced on a clean tree 2026-06-12 — pre-existing environment issue, not a test bug. Don't burn time debugging the test itself; verify date-filter logic standalone or run in CI. Other suites (dropbox, config, readonly, tool-surface, server) run fine.
tests/features/git-history.test.ts.author: agent

src/control/consumer.ts.note: Three load-bearing quirks: (1) macOS fs.watch reports a rename-into-place ONLY under the SOURCE name (`<id>.json.tmp`), never the target — so any event triggers a full dir rescan (enqueuePending), never filename filtering. (2) fs.watch is best-effort AND macOS FSEvents activates asynchronously: a request landing between the startup drain and watcher activation gets no event. The post-registration enqueuePending() call + 2s poll interval are the delivery guarantee — do not remove them as "redundant". (3) drain() starts with `await Promise.resolve()` on purpose: with an empty queue the async body would otherwise complete synchronously and run `finally { drainPromise = null }` BEFORE the assignment lands, permanently wedging the queue. Same drain pattern in startConversationFolderWatch (src/conversation/indexer.ts).
src/control/consumer.ts.author: agent

src/server/index.ts.note: The applyEmbeddingConfigFromDisk(startupDir) call AFTER the connectedRepos warm-attach loop is load-bearing: each readonly attach reconfigures the GLOBAL embedder to that repo's model (its ctor compat asserts need it). Without the restore, startup indexing of the primary embeds with the LAST connected repo's model — wrong-dim/garbage vectors in the primary index. Don't remove it or move the attach loop after startIndexingWork without re-checking this.
src/server/index.ts.author: agent

src/db/index.ts.note: Readonly mode (opts.readonly, used for connect_repo cross-repo attach) must NEVER gain writes: the foreign repo's own — possibly older — server owns the file, and a migration/stamp under a live writer is the corruption this mode exists to prevent. Hidden-write traps already handled: logQuery no-ops on readonly (search logs analytics!), normalizeAnnotationPaths/initSchema/recordEmbeddingModel/WAL-pragma all skipped in the readonly ctor branch. If you add a new write that can be reached from a read tool path, guard it with this.isReadonly.
src/db/index.ts.author: agent

src/conversation/indexer.ts.note: drain()'s leading `await Promise.resolve()` is load-bearing: with an empty queue (project with no transcripts yet) the async body completes synchronously and `finally { drainPromise = null }` would run BEFORE the assignment lands, leaving drainPromise stuck on a resolved promise — every later watcher event would no-op and live indexing would silently stop. Same pattern + same reason in src/control/consumer.ts startCommandDropbox.
src/conversation/indexer.ts.author: agent

src/conversation/indexer.ts.note: VERIFIED BUG (2026-06-10, empirical /tmp harness): the legacy-offset upgrade probe (isRewoundLastTurn, ~lines 65-71) compares ONLY userText. With a legacy EOF-semantics offset AND a new turn whose user text equals the stored last turn's (common: "continue"), the probe mis-binds — the new turn REPLACES the stored last turn instead of appending. One turn is lost and session tokens undercount. A from-0 re-parse fully recovers (deleteTurnsAbove reconciliation works). All other paths verified clean: mid-turn splits at every line boundary, gapped legacy indices, tool-result-only continuations, foreign-cwd interleave, mid-pass appends; embed churn bounded. Fix direction: strengthen the probe (also require stored assistantText to be a prefix of the re-parsed turn's, or persist/compare the last turn's startByteOffset).
src/conversation/indexer.ts.author: agent

src/cli/commands/conversation.ts.note: Latent race: this CLI calls indexConversation directly with no index lock. The server's conversation folder watcher serializes all indexConversation runs through one queue because overlapping runs on the same transcript can corrupt turn_count (see src/conversation/indexer.ts:253-265). Running this command while a live server holds the lock bypasses that queue. Planned fix: 3-step fallback (acquire lock → reclaim stale → drop-box command to holder) in plans/command-dropbox.md.
src/cli/commands/conversation.ts.author: agent

src/embeddings/embed.ts.note: Do NOT raise this default for throughput. History: shipped at cores/2, users complained indexing made machines unusable while working; lowered to cores/3 deliberately. Benchmarks WILL show 1.5-2x index speedup at 6-8 threads (measured 2026-06-10: 2t=19.4ms/chunk, 6t=9.4, 8t=9.0 on 10-core M-series) — that's the trap. Day-to-day machine impact wins over index speed for the default; per-project config.indexThreads is the escape hatch for users who want a fast foreground index.
src/embeddings/embed.ts.author: agent

benchmarks/contextbench/graph-completene.note: call✓ metric (resolved/linkable symbol refs) is COLLISION-INFLATED — do not read it as "call graph 70% incomplete". Drill-down (callgraph-miss-probe.ts on matplotlib): ~99% of unresolved-linkable refs are NO_IMPORT (file binds no import of that name); only ~1% are import-cascade misses. Of NO_IMPORT, ~half are ambiguous common names and the "unique-exporter" half is ALSO mostly noise: local vars / builtins (line, item, type, object, path, range) and method/attribute dispatch (ax.plot, fig.legend → matches pyplot.plot export) that share a name with one project export. Resolving those needs receiver TYPE INFERENCE, which mimirs deliberately won't do (static, zero-LLM). Conclusion: the resolver already captures ~all statically-recoverable edges; call-graph "incompleteness" is not a fixable gap. Import graph (~98%) is the real asset — lean retrieval levers on imports, not the symbol/call graph (explains why callee-expand-lab was weak).
benchmarks/contextbench/graph-completene.author: agent

src/search/hybrid.ts.note: Twin scoring bug (still UNFIXED here, fixed in searchChunks' inline affinity 2026-06-07): (1) stemWords = stem.split(/[_-]+/) has no length guard, so a stem like "__init__" -> ["","init",""] and the empty string matches EVERY query word via qw.includes("") -> stemMatchCount explodes the multiplier (saw 129x on a long query, rocketing __init__.py to rank 1). (2) queryWords not deduped -> filename affinity inflated by term frequency. Effect scales with query length: catastrophic for long issue text (ContextBench), mild for short topic queries (why internal recall@K didn't visibly break). Fix mirrors searchChunks: stemWords.filter(w=>w.length>=3) + [...new Set(queryWords)]. Left unfixed because this feeds file-level search() (internal recall@K benchmark), needs re-verification there — out of scope for the ContextBench-only scoring goal.
src/search/hybrid.ts.author: agent
